Let $T(n)$ be the number of incongruent triangles with integral sides and
perimeter $n \ge 6$. Prove that $T(n) = T(n - 3)$ if $n$ is even, or disprove
by a counterexample. (Note: two triangles are {\it congruent} if there is a
one-to-one correspondence between the sides of the two triangles such that
corresponding sides have the same length.)
